h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: The gap keeps 12.01 separate from carbon. Top 6 atomic number, C symbol, carbon name, bottom 12.01 atomic mass. I will copy that order.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Parking place only; this card has to pass alone.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to copy all four labels together in the established top-to-bottom order once the layout was confirmed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: takes the atomic number card from the upside-down tray and sets it beside the top 6 … Atomic number points to the top 6 only, so it can move to the copy side. The other cards stay on the tray; next I test symbol against C, not copy the whole row together.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the new one-card-at-a-time verification criterion, names the match, moves the card to the copy side, and explicitly defers the remaining cards — performing the new device rather than merely describing it.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The tray line stopped settling this; it made a false row. Now it is only a parking place. One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side. No card copies until it passes alone.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly names the failure of the prior tray-line device ('stopped settling this; it made a false row') and replaces it with a sequenced one-card verification gate — a structurally different task that forces individual matching before any copying, satisfying both the stock-taking contrast and the new public mechanism requirements.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side. No card copies until it passes alone.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precisely fitted to the diagnosed failure (false-row batch copying), gives the learner a concrete, repeatable three-step criterion (lift, place, name, move), is visibly different from the prior layout-and-gap route, and transfers directly to each remaining card — high specificity, learner agency, and economy.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Take only the symbol card next and set it beside C. If it fits alone, move it to the copy side; the other cards wait their own turn before the packet copy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 responds directly to L4's enacted use of the new criterion by naming the next specific card and reinforcing the 'alone' gate, adapting the task forward from the learner's revised frame rather than returning to the original lesson plan.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The upward one should be table on the cart, if the table is what is pushing up.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The rolling direction is not what fixes the label.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply read the visible card text and place it by its arrow, continuing the straightforward labelling routine.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: puts the contact strip under the rightward arrow and moves the rolling-left strip into the side space
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 physically enacts the interaction/motion distinction by sorting the two strips into separate spaces, performing the new classification criterion rather than merely asserting it.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly marks the old route as incomplete ('has not yet tested the motion case') and introduces a new sorting device with a dedicated 'not a force label' space, giving the learner a different physical task that separates interaction from motion description.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The strip-sorting device is precisely fitted to L2's confusion between force-source and motion-direction, gives the learner a concrete spatial criterion to apply independently, and is visibly different from the prior card-placement routine.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 acknowledges the learner's motion-direction question from L2 and reframes the task around it, but the response is more a continuation of the sorting mechanism than a further adaptation to the learner's revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 4 still looks like it could control how fast the counters move.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: so the 4 stays checked under the quarter-note tile as the beat-unit label
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue restating the three-part formula until the worksheet was fully settled.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: with the 3/4 boxes covered, writes "speed" in the tempo-mark space. This side answers speed, not beat count or one beat unit, so the 4 stays checked under the quarter-note tile as the beat-unit label.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively applies the covered-area test introduced by the tutor, assigning 'speed' to the tempo space and using that spatial result to confirm the beat-unit label — a new criterion enacted on the page rather than merely declared.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: that label check is not settling the look of the 4. This boundary has to test what each area is allowed to answer. With the tempo space covered, mark which visible area can answer: beat count, one beat unit, speed. [slides the cover over the 3/4 boxes and leaves the tempo space open.] Now run the same three prompts here. If speed only has a place on this side, the 4 stays a beat-unit label, not a speed control.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names that the existing label-check route has failed ('not settling the look of the 4') and introduces a new spatial-occlusion device — covering each area in turn and running the three prompts — that forces the learner to test jurisdictions rather than repeat the formula, satisfying both the stock-taking contrast and the new public mechanism requirements.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: With the tempo space covered, mark which visible area can answer: beat count, one beat unit, speed. [slides the cover over the 3/4 boxes and leaves the tempo space open.] Now run the same three prompts here. If speed only has a place on this side, the 4 stays a beat-unit label, not a speed control.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The occlusion device is precisely fitted to the learner's stated blockage (the 4 'looks like' it could control speed) by making the two areas mutually exclusive and giving the learner an actionable spatial criterion — cover one region, run the prompts, see what remains — rather than repeating the verbal formula or completing the task for the learner.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: beat count in the bar; note value that gets one beat. Bar line after three counters. Quarter-note tile under the 4. Neither number gives speed; speed stays in the tempo space.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 closes by restating the settled formula and confirming the object positions, but does not visibly adapt to or build on the learner's revised framing from L4 — it is a consolidating summary rather than a response to the learner's new spatial reasoning.
